Veterans Goals: I broke my back during a fall I had in the Navy I have buddy letters from fellow service members and a letter from a medical professional stating my injury happened during service. The problem is my HM1 at the time did not record my fall or anything relevant to it in my medical record and I assumed he knew what he was talking about when he said it was a sprain and gave me ibuprofen. Shortly after the incident (about 5 months) I was discharged and my back continued to hurt. A year after discharge I had a major pain increase and went to the emergency room where I found that I have chronic stensis in my thoracic spine as well as two permanent compression fractures in my vertebrae. I know that my case may be stacked against me but this has affected my life tremendously as I am no longer able to do many things I could do before. I am trying my best I have submitted an initial claim a supplemental claim and a higher review for this and each time the VA has come back and said it’s not service connected. I know it was the fall down my ships hazmat ladderwell that caused this injury and I don’t know what to do please help.
